text: BHARAT SURAKSHA MOTOR INSURANCE CO. LTD MOTOR PRIVATE CAR COMPREHENSIVE POLICY WORDINGS Policy Type: Comprehensive Own Damage and Third Party Liability Whereas the insured, by a proposal and declaration dated as stated in the Schedule, has applied to Bharat Suraksha
Motor Insurance Company Limited (hereinafter referred to as the Company) for the insurance described herein, and has paid the premium stated in the Schedule as consideration for this insurance.

SECTION I. LOSS OF OR DAMAGE TO THE VEHICLE INSURED The Company will indemnify the insured against loss or damage to the vehicle insured and its accessories whilst thereon, arising from the following perils: (cid:127) By fire, explosion, self-ignition, or lightning.

(cid:127) By burglary, housebreaking, or theft. (cid:127) By riot and strike. (cid:127) By earthquake, including fire and shock damage. (cid:127) By flood, typhoon, hurricane, storm, tempest, inundation, cyclone, hailstorm, or frost.

(cid:127) By accidental external means, including collision, impact with another vehicle or stationary object, and resulting dents, scratches, cracks, and breakage of lamps or other exterior fittings.

(cid:127) By malicious act, including intentional scratching, keying, or deliberate damage to exterior panels. (cid:127) By terrorist activity. (cid:127) Whilst in transit by road, rail, inland waterway, lift, elevator, or air. (cid:127) By landslide or rockslide. DEPRECIATION ON REPLACED PARTS

The following depreciation rates apply to parts necessarily replaced: (cid:127) For all rubber, nylon, plastic parts, tyres, tubes, batteries, and air bags: 50% depreciation. (cid:127) For fibre glass components: 30% depreciation.

(cid:127) For all parts made of glass, including windscreen, rear windscreen, and door windows: Nil depreciation applies. The full replacement cost is recoverable. (cid:127) For all other parts including wooden parts: depreciation as per the schedule below. Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation

Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation Not exceeding 6 months Nil Exceeding 6 months but not exceeding 1 year 5% Exceeding 1 year but not exceeding 2 years 10% Exceeding 2 years but not exceeding 3 years 15% Exceeding 3 years but not exceeding 4 years 25% Exceeding 4 years but not exceeding 5 years 35%
Exceeding 5 years but not exceeding 10 years 40% Exceeding 10 years 50% Rate of Depreciation for Painting: A depreciation rate of 50% is applied only on the material cost of total painting

charges. In the case of a consolidated bill, the material component is deemed to be 25% of total painting charges. TYRE AND TUBE DAMAGE Damage to tyres and tubes is covered subject to a deduction of 50% of the cost of replacement, but only if the vehicle
insured has also sustained damage at the same time. Where the vehicle has not been damaged simultaneously, tyre and tube damage alone is not recoverable under this section. EXCLUSIONS UNDER SECTION I
The Company shall not be liable to make any payment in respect of:
1. Consequential loss, depreciation, wear and tear, or mechanical and electrical breakdown, failures, or breakages, even where such breakdown results in external visible damage.

2. Dents, scratches, or panel damage arising solely from gradual deterioration, road vibration over time, or corrosion, rather than from a specific identifiable accidental event covered under this policy.
3. Scratch or abrasion damage attributable to routine car washing, polishing, or normal servicing activities, classified as wear and tear.
4. Cracks in the dashboard, door trims, or interior plastic components from thermal expansion or contraction due to ambient temperature changes.
5. Lamp failure due to bulb burnout, filament failure, or electrical fault, without any accompanying physical impact or breakage of the lamp housing or lens.
6. Condensation or fogging inside lamp assemblies not accompanied by physical damage to the lamp housing itself.
7. Windscreen scratches or minor surface marks on glass that do not constitute a break, crack, or chip impairing the driver's field of vision.
8. Tyre wear and tread depth below the legal minimum limit, or damage arising from driving on tyres in an unroadworthy condition.
9. Tyre or tube damage when the vehicle was being operated outside the geographical area specified in the Schedule at the time of loss.
10. Loss or damage arising from contractual liability or arising out of use of the vehicle otherwise than in accordance with the Limitations as to Use.
11. Any loss or damage sustained whilst the insured or driver was under the influence of intoxicating liquor or drugs. BHARAT SURAKSHA MOTOR INSURANCE CO. LTD

SECTION II. SUM INSURED AND INSURED DECLARED VALUE (IDV) The Insured Declared Value (IDV) of the vehicle shall be the Sum Insured for the purpose of this policy, fixed at policy commencement based on the manufacturer's listed selling price adjusted for depreciation as per the schedule below:

Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation Not exceeding 6 months 5% Exceeding 6 months but not exceeding 1 year 15% Exceeding 1 year but not exceeding 2 years 20% Exceeding 2 years but not exceeding 3 years 30% Exceeding 3 years but not exceeding 4 years 40% Exceeding 4 years but not exceeding 5 years 50%
The insured vehicle shall be treated as a Constructive Total Loss if the aggregate cost of retrieval and repair exceeds 75% of the IDV.

SECTION III. DEDUCTIBLE The Company shall not be liable for the first Rs. 1,000 of each and every claim under Section I of this policy. This deductible applies to each occurrence independently, including occurrences giving rise to a Total Loss or Constructive Total Loss claim.

SECTION IV. THIRD PARTY LIABILITY Subject to the limits of liability in the Schedule, the Company will indemnify the insured against all sums the insured becomes legally liable to pay in respect of death of or bodily injury to any person, or damage to third-party property,

caused by or arising from use of the vehicle, as required under the Motor Vehicles Act. Provided always that the Company shall not be liable for death, injury, or property damage arising beyond the limits of any public carriageway in connection with loading or unloading of the vehicle.

SECTION V. ADD-ON COVERS Add-On 1: Nil Depreciation Cover (Zero Dep) In the event of a partial loss claim, no depreciation deduction shall apply to the cost of parts replaced, subject to payment of additional premium. This cover is available for vehicles not exceeding 5 years from date of first

registration. Add-On 2: Roadside Assistance Emergency towing assistance is provided up to 50 km free of charge in the event of accident or breakdown. Flat tyre replacement using the vehicle's stepney is included. Fuel delivery of up to 5 litres is chargeable at actual cost.

Add-On 3: Engine Protection Cover Consequential damage to engine parts and gearbox components arising from water ingression due to flood or inundation is covered under this add-on, subject to the claim under Section I being admissible and the vehicle being

retrieved from the waterlogged area within 72 hours. Exclusion under Engine Protection: Damage to engine or gearbox arising from delay in intimation, from driving the vehicle after it has ingested water, or from gradual corrosion of engine parts, is not covered.

A No Claim Bonus discount is applicable on Own Damage premium at renewal: Claim History NCB Discount on OD Premium 1 claim-free year 20% 2 consecutive claim-free years 25% 3 consecutive claim-free years 35% 4 consecutive claim-free years 45% 5 or more consecutive claim-free years 50%
NCB reverts to 0% at the next renewal if any claim is made during the policy period.
1. The insured must give immediate written notice to the Company upon the occurrence of any accidental loss or damage. In the case of theft, immediate notice to the police is also required.
2. No admission, offer, or promise of payment shall be made by the insured without the written consent of the Company.
3. The insured shall take all reasonable steps to safeguard the vehicle from further loss or damage after an accident.
4. If a dispute arises as to the quantum payable under this policy, the matter shall be referred to arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.
5. The due observance of all terms, conditions, and endorsements of this policy is a condition precedent to any liability of the Company.

SECTION VIII. CLAIM PROCEDURE (cid:127) Intimate the claim to the Company within 48 hours of the incident. (cid:127) Do not move or repair the vehicle until a surveyor has been appointed, except for essential emergency repairs not exceeding Rs. 500.

exceeding Rs. 500. (cid:127) Submit completed claim form, RC copy, driving licence, FIR (if applicable), and repair estimate. (cid:127) Payment will be made within 7 working days of completion of documentation.

SECTION IX. GENERAL EXCEPTIONS This policy does not cover loss, damage, or liability arising from nuclear weapons, ionising radiation, war, invasion, civil war, mutiny, or warlike operations. Claims where the vehicle was being used outside the permitted geographical

area, or in violation of the Driver's Clause, are also excluded. Insurance is the subject matter of solicitation. SAFEDRIVE ASSURANCE CORPORATION PRIVATE MOTOR VEHICLE OWN DAMAGE POLICY Standalone Own Damage Cover with Nil Depreciation Add-On This policy document sets out the full terms and conditions of the insurance contract between the insured named in the
Schedule and SafeDrive Assurance Corporation. The Schedule, this document, and any endorsements together form the entire policy agreement. PART A. WHAT WE COVER - OWN DAMAGE

We will pay for physical loss or damage to your vehicle caused by: (cid:127) Fire, explosion, lightning, or self-ignition. (cid:127) Theft, burglary, or housebreaking. (cid:127) Riots, strikes, or civil disturbance. (cid:127) Earthquake, flood, cyclone, hailstorm, or other natural calamity.

(cid:127) Physical impact damage caused by collision with another vehicle, animal, or fixed object, resulting in dents to panels, bumpers, or wheel arches. (cid:127) Surface damage including paint and body abrasions arising from collision, intentional acts, or contact with

abrasive surfaces where an identifiable accidental event can be established. (cid:127) Structural fractures to body panels, bumpers, and exterior trim resulting from impact, collision, or accidental contact.

contact. (cid:127) Damage to lighting units including headlamp assemblies, tail lamp clusters, fog lamps, indicator units, and reflectors, caused by impact or accident. (cid:127) Windscreen, rear windscreen, and door window glass damage - glass is covered at nil depreciation. The full

cost of windscreen replacement is payable without any deduction for the age of the vehicle. (cid:127) Landslide or rockslide. (cid:127) Transit damage during transportation by road, rail, or sea. PART A1. GLASS COVERAGE DETAILS

PART A1. GLASS COVERAGE DETAILS Glass components of the vehicle including the front windscreen, rear windscreen, quarter glass panels, and door windows are covered without any depreciation deduction regardless of vehicle age. This means the full invoice cost of
glass replacement at an authorised service centre is recoverable.

What is NOT covered under glass: (cid:127) Internal rear-view mirror glass that is not part of the vehicle's main glazing. (cid:127) Sunroof and panoramic roof glass, unless specifically endorsed on the Schedule.

(cid:127) Superficial glass scratches or micro-abrasions that do not constitute a break or chip. (cid:127) Glass damage sustained while the vehicle is in commercial transit not covered under this policy. (cid:127) Tinted film or protective coatings applied to glass after vehicle manufacture.

's specified zero-pressure range. al racing, rallying, or off-road purposes not permitted under e or tube damage. re, ozone degradation, or age. SAFEDRIVE ASSURANCE CORPORATION PART B. NIL DEPRECIATION ADD-ON (INCLUDED IN THIS POLICY)

This policy includes Nil Depreciation cover as a standard feature for vehicles not exceeding 3 years from the date of first registration. For vehicles aged 3 to 5 years, a 75% waiver of applicable depreciation is provided. No nil depreciation benefit is available for vehicles older than 5 years.

Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation Vehicle age up to 3 years Nil depreciation (100% parts cost recoverable) Vehicle age 3 to 5 years 75% waiver of standard depreciation Vehicle age exceeding 5 years Standard depreciation applies as per schedule PART C. WHAT WE DO NOT COVER
The following are excluded from this policy:
1. Consequential financial loss, loss of use, or reduction in market value of the vehicle following an accident, even if the physical damage is covered.
2. Panel dents, scratches, or marks arising from gradual accumulation of minor incidents over time where no single identifiable accident can be attributed as the cause.
3. Scratch damage resulting from routine washing and cleaning activities of the vehicle, classified under fair wear and tear.
4. Cracks in engine mountings, chassis members, or structural frame components arising from metal fatigue or prolonged vibration.
5. Lamp malfunction due to electrical failure, water ingression through an undamaged seal, or simple bulb failure without physical damage to the lamp body.
6. Corrosion of lamp housings or lighting units from long-term exposure to the elements, classified as deterioration rather than accidental damage.
7. Glass haze, delamination, or yellowing of polycarbonate headlamp covers from UV exposure over time, not caused by a specific impact event.
8. Damage to tyres used beyond their rated load index or speed rating as specified by the vehicle manufacturer.
9. Any claim where the vehicle was driven with a tyre already known to be flat or significantly underinflated at the time of the journey.
10. Mechanical or electrical breakdown of any component, including the engine, gearbox, suspension, or braking system, unless caused directly by an insured peril. PART D. INSURED DECLARED VALUE (IDV)

PART D. INSURED DECLARED VALUE (IDV) Your vehicle's IDV represents the maximum we will pay in the event of a total loss or theft. It is calculated at the start of the policy using the manufacturer's list price reduced by depreciation based on vehicle age: Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation

Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation Not exceeding 6 months 5% Exceeding 6 months but not exceeding 1 year 15% Exceeding 1 year but not exceeding 2 years 20% Exceeding 2 years but not exceeding 3 years 30% Exceeding 3 years but not exceeding 4 years 40% Exceeding 4 years but not exceeding 5 years 50%

A claim will be settled as Total Loss if the cost of repair exceeds 75% of IDV. PART E. DEDUCTIBLE You are responsible for the first Rs. 2,000 of each own damage claim under Part A of this policy. This compulsory

excess applies per occurrence. An optional voluntary excess of Rs. 1,000, Rs. 2,000, or Rs. 5,000 may be selected at inception for a premium discount. PART F. NO CLAIM BONUS Claim History NCB at Renewal NCB if Claim Made 0 claims in preceding year 20% 0% 0 claims in preceding 2 years 25% 0%

0 claims in preceding 2 years 25% 0% 0 claims in preceding 3 years 35% 0% 0 claims in preceding 4 years 45% 0% 0 claims in preceding 5+ years 50% 0% PART G. HOW TO MAKE A CLAIM (cid:127) Contact our 24-hour claims helpline within 24 hours of the incident.

(cid:127) Do not authorise repairs above Rs. 500 without our written approval. (cid:127) Submit your claim form, vehicle documents, and repair estimate within 7 days. (cid:127) Payment is processed within 5 working days of survey completion. PART H. GENERAL EXCEPTIONS

PART H. GENERAL EXCEPTIONS This policy does not cover any loss arising from: war, nuclear risk, use of vehicle in violation of permit conditions, driving without a valid licence, or use of the vehicle outside the permitted geographical area.

QUICKCLAIM GENERAL INSURANCE LIMITED MOTOR VEHICLE PACKAGE POLICY - SCHEDULE OF TERMS AND CONDITIONS Comprehensive Own Damage and Compulsory Third Party Cover The insured, having submitted a duly completed proposal form, is hereby granted insurance by QuickClaim General
Insurance Limited, subject to the terms, exclusions, conditions, and limitations set forth herein. The Schedule attached forms an integral part of this contract. OPERATIVE CLAUSE - OWN DAMAGE SECTION
OPERATIVE CLAUSE - OWN DAMAGE SECTION The Company undertakes to indemnify the insured in respect of accidental loss or damage to the vehicle insured,

arising from and directly caused by the following events during the policy period: (cid:127) Fire, self-ignition, lightning, explosion. (cid:127) Theft, robbery, or wrongful abstraction. (cid:127) Riot, strike, or civil commotion. (cid:127) Earthquake fire and shock damage.

(cid:127) Flood, cyclone, hurricane, inundation, hailstorm. (cid:127) Damage arising from accidental contact with another vehicle or stationary object, resulting in deformation of body panels, wheel arches, bumpers, and associated exterior surfaces. This includes dents of all sizes caused by

such contact. (cid:127) Damage arising from contact with abrasive surfaces or deliberate acts amounting to malicious mischief, where an act by an identifiable third party can be established and where the damage manifests as surface scratches or paint abrasion.

scratches or paint abrasion. (cid:127) Damage to plastic, composite, or metal components resulting in fracture or structural breach, caused directly by impact from an insured peril, specifically including cracks to bumpers, wheel arch liners, door panels, and sills.

and sills. (cid:127) Destruction or breakage of headlamps, tail lamps, indicator assemblies, and auxiliary lamps caused directly by mechanical impact during an insured event. (cid:127) Shattering, cracking, or breaking of any glass component of the vehicle, including the front windscreen, rear

windscreen, and side door glass. Glass is indemnified at actual replacement cost without depreciation deduction. (cid:127) Landslide, rockslide. (cid:127) Transit damage. SPECIAL CONDITIONS - TYRE DAMAGE

SPECIAL CONDITIONS - TYRE DAMAGE Loss or damage to tyres and inner tubes is covered subject to a deduction of 50% of the cost of replacement, provided that the vehicle insured has sustained concurrent physical damage in the same incident. Coverage under this clause

is conditional upon the simultaneous presence of other admissible damage to the vehicle body. SCHEDULE OF EXCLUDED LOSSES AND LIABILITIES Notwithstanding the operative clause above, the Company shall in no event be liable under this policy for any of the

following: A. Exclusions Relating to Panel and Body Damage A1. Dents or deformations of body panels arising from metal fatigue, long-term vibration stress, or corrosion from within, even where the resulting deformation is externally visible.

A2. Panel damage attributable to overloading of the vehicle beyond the manufacturer's gross vehicle weight rating, where such overloading is the proximate cause. A3. Dents or scratches on body components that existed prior to the inception of this policy, as documented in the pre-inspection report.

pre-inspection report. A4. Cosmetic marks or shallow surface scuffs on bumpers caused by parking in proximity to kerb stones during normal driving, classified as unavoidable wear. A5. Body damage arising from manufacturer recall campaigns or factory defects, recoverable instead under the

manufacturer's warranty. B. Exclusions Relating to Scratch and Paint Damage B1. Scratches and paint damage from automated car wash equipment, hand washing with abrasive materials, or application of improper cleaning agents.

B2. Fade, oxidation, or chalking of paintwork from prolonged UV exposure or environmental pollutants, constituting gradual deterioration. B3. Paint damage arising from bird droppings, tree sap, or industrial fallout, not caused by a sudden and identifiable insured event.

insured event. B4. Scratches on internally fitted surfaces, door sills, or boot lids from loading and unloading of goods by the insured. B5. Any scratch or paint damage not reported to the Company within 30 days of occurrence, unless the insured provides written justification for the delay.

C. Exclusions Relating to Cracks C1. Hairline cracks in laminated safety glass that do not progress into full fractures and do not impair the driver's field of vision as defined in Rule 100 of the Central Motor Vehicles Rules, 1989.

C2. Cracks in engine block, cylinder head, or other internal mechanical components, even where resulting from an external impact, unless such impact is also separately claimed under the body damage section.

C3. Cracks in dashboard, door card, or interior trim materials from heat, UV exposure, or age-related embrittlement. C4. Fractures to tow bar attachments, roof rack fittings, or aftermarket accessories not listed in the original manufacturer's specification.

manufacturer's specification. C5. Stress cracks in the vehicle chassis or monocoque arising from prolonged use on uneven roads, classified as structural fatigue. QUICKCLAIM GENERAL INSURANCE LIMITED D. Exclusions Relating to Lamp Damage

D. Exclusions Relating to Lamp Damage D1. Failure of lamp units due to electrical faults in the vehicle wiring, including short circuits, voltage spikes, or faulty relays, without concurrent physical damage to the lamp housing.

D2. Lamp damage arising from thermal stress caused by the use of non-OEM replacement bulbs with wattage exceeding the manufacturer's specification. D3. Damage to interior cabin lighting, instrument cluster lighting, or decorative LED strips not forming part of the vehicle's regulatory lighting.

vehicle's regulatory lighting. D4. Discolouration, cloudiness, or UV hazing of polycarbonate headlamp covers, not resulting from a specific impact event. D5. Lamp assembly damage to vehicles modified for commercial hire or rental use, contrary to the permitted use under this policy.
under this policy. E. Exclusions Relating to Glass Damage E1. Glass damage sustained on vehicles being transported as commercial cargo, unless transit is specifically endorsed on the Schedule.

endorsed on the Schedule. E2. Replacement or repair of tinted films, UV filters, or decorative coatings applied to glass surfaces post-manufacture. E3. Sunroof or glass roof panel damage unless specifically included by endorsement in the Schedule.

E4. Microscopic surface scratches on tempered side glass that do not impair visibility or constitute a structural breach. E5. Glass repair (as distinct from replacement) where the repaired area exceeds the diameter threshold specified in the Surveyor's Guidelines issued by IRDAI from time to time.

F. Exclusions Relating to Tyre Damage F1. Tyre damage occurring when the vehicle is operated outside the permitted geographical area stated in the Schedule. F2. Destruction of tyres from illegal motor racing, rallying, or competitive driving events.

F3. Tyre valve stem damage alone, without accompanying damage to the tyre carcass, bead, or inner tube. F4. Damage to run-flat tyres operated beyond zero-pressure range exceeding the manufacturer's specified distance limit.

limit. F5. Tyre damage resulting from driving over road conditions that the insured was aware of before commencing the journey. DEDUCTIBLE The insured shall bear the first Rs. 1,500 of each and every own damage claim. This compulsory deductible is not insurable. IDV AND TOTAL LOSS

Exceeding 4 years but not exceeding 5 years 50% Total Loss is declared when repair cost exceeds 75% of IDV. CLAIM INTIMATION Intimation of any claim must be made to the Company within 48 hours of the occurrence. Failure to comply with this
condition may prejudice the insured's entitlement to indemnity. This is a synthetic specimen policy for research and educational use only. Not a valid insurance contract.

Add-On A: Nil Depreciation Available for vehicles up to 5 years old. No depreciation is charged on parts replaced during a partial loss claim, subject to maximum two claims per policy year. Add-On B: Return to Invoice

VALUEMOTOR COMPREHENSIVE INSURANCE LTD VALUEMOTOR COMPREHENSIVE MOTOR POLICY Affordable Comprehensive Own Damage and Third Party Cover Thank you for choosing ValueMotor Comprehensive Insurance. This document explains what is and is not covered
under your policy. Please read it carefully and keep it safe.

Your vehicle is covered for loss or damage caused by: (cid:127) Fire, explosion, or lightning strike. (cid:127) Theft or attempted theft. (cid:127) Riots, strikes, or acts of terrorism. (cid:127) Natural disasters: floods, cyclones, earthquakes, hailstorms.

(cid:127) Accidental collision or impact - loss or damage caused by your vehicle colliding with another vehicle or object, resulting in dents to the vehicle body, bumpers, or wheel arches. (cid:127) Cosmetic damage from accident or vandalism - scratching, keying, or paint abrasion caused by an

identifiable accidental or malicious event. (cid:127) Cracking or fracturing of exterior components by accidental means - this includes cracks in bumpers, door panels, trims, and sills caused by impact.

(cid:127) Breakage of headlight, taillight, or indicator units by accident or collision during the policy period. (cid:127) Glass damage without depreciation deduction - your windscreen, rear windscreen, and door windows are covered at full replacement cost as standard.

(cid:127) Tyre and tube damage at 50% of replacement cost, only when the vehicle has also been damaged in the same incident. (cid:127) Damage in transit by road, rail, or water. (cid:127) Landslide or rockslide damage.

2. HOW DEPRECIATION WORKS When a part is replaced, we deduct a percentage for depreciation based on your vehicle's age, except for glass (always nil depreciation): Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation Not exceeding 6 months Nil Exceeding 6 months but not exceeding 1 year 5%

4. YOUR EXCESS (DEDUCTIBLE) You pay the first Rs. 1,000 of each claim. This is your compulsory excess. You cannot insure this amount with us or any other insurer.

5. INSURED DECLARED VALUE AND TOTAL LOSS The IDV is your vehicle's sum insured. It is based on the manufacturer's ex-showroom price reduced for age: Age of Vehicle % of Depreciation Not exceeding 6 months 5% Exceeding 6 months but not exceeding 1 year 15%
Exceeding 1 year but not exceeding 2 years 20% Exceeding 2 years but not exceeding 3 years 30% Exceeding 3 years but not exceeding 4 years 40% Exceeding 4 years but not exceeding 5 years 50%
If your repair cost exceeds 75% of the IDV, we will settle your claim as a Total Loss and pay you the IDV less any salvage value.

6. NO CLAIM BONUS Your claim history Your NCB discount 1 claim-free year 20% 2 claim-free years 25% 3 claim-free years 35% 4 claim-free years 45% 5+ claim-free years 50% Your NCB resets to 0% if you make a claim in any year.
